title: "You'll need journalism so distinctive it has its own gravity": NYT publisher A.G. Sulzberger on how news organizations can stand up to AI companies
tldr: Sulzberger's WAN-IFRA keynote argues that distinctiveness — not scale — is the only defense against AI commoditization of journalism. A sharp framing of the differentiation imperative for any content business.
why_fresh: new strategic framing from a major industry voice
angle: What 'gravity' means for brand content: why distinctiveness beats volume in an AI-saturated feed.

title: The Future of Content Belongs to the Tastemakers
tldr: Argues that with AI making polished copy trivial, editorial taste and curation become the real differentiator. Directly useful for Igor's thinking on content value post-AI.
why_fresh: reframes content value around taste, not production
angle: Why the scarcest content skill in 2026 is judgment, not output.

title: Your Content Isn't Just Competing With Other Brands Anymore
tldr: In AI discovery environments, content competes with AI summaries and answer engines, not just rival brands. Breaks down what that means for SEO and share of voice.
why_fresh: reframes competitive landscape around AI answer engines
angle: How to audit your content for the post-search, answer-engine era.

title: 'Social media is now three distinct categories'
tldr: Researchers argue platforms have split into broadcasters, micro-communities, and AI-driven experiences — a structural reframing of social media strategy. Rich fodder for Igor's audience strategy thinking.
angle: Rebuilding your social strategy around three distinct platform logics.

title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: As AI makes production cheap, editorial judgment becomes the scarce asset — making the managing editor the pivotal content-team hire. A clear ops argument.
why_fresh: new org-design argument for the AI era
angle: Why the managing editor is the most undervalued role in the AI content stack.

title: Coca-Cola's AI-powered José Mourinho campaign could signal a shift in celebrity partnerships
tldr: Coke replaces the real Mourinho with his AI clone as campaign host — a possible inflection point for synthetic celebrity endorsements. Concrete signal of synthetic media going mainstream.
why_fresh: synthetic celebrity goes mainstream in a major campaign
angle: What AI clones of public figures mean for endorsement ethics and brand control.

title: Why Duluth trusts AI agents with bidding, but not brand storytelling
tldr: Duluth Trading draws a clear line: automate media buying with AI agents, keep humans on the narrative. A useful real-world map of where to deploy AI in content work.
angle: Drawing the line: which content tasks to automate and which to protect.

title: 3 questions to ask before putting your CEO in front of the media during a crisis
tldr: FleishmanHillard's Elizabeth Cook on why a crisis is the wrong time to expose CEO weaknesses, and how to decide spokesperson readiness. Practical crisis-comms guidance.
angle: A readiness checklist for deciding whether your CEO should face the cameras.

title: How to prepare for more declines in traditional Google search traffic
tldr: With AI Overviews suppressing clicks, experts share tactics for maintaining visibility when AI supplies the answers. Directly relevant to content discovery strategy.
why_fresh: actionable response to the AI-search traffic shift
angle: Building a visibility strategy for a zero-click, AI-answer world.

title: Layoffs don't have to feel inhumane
tldr: Reframes layoffs from a messaging problem to a trust moment, arguing employee evaluation of leadership is now near-instant. Strong internal-comms and change-management angle.
why_fresh: reframes layoffs as a trust/comms problem, not messaging
angle: How layoff communications reveal whether leadership can still be trusted.

title: AI 'content creators' are getting harder to spot
tldr: The Verge tracks how AI influencers have become nearly indistinguishable from humans, raising authenticity and disclosure questions for the creator economy.
angle: The authenticity crisis: what happens to trust when you can't tell who's real.

title: Why 'AI Productivity Gains' Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Argues productivity framing fails to move CMOs, CFOs and legal — and shows how to reframe AI metrics per audience to defend budget and headcount. Smart internal-persuasion playbook.
angle: How to translate AI value into the language each C-suite stakeholder actually buys.

title: AI Is Quietly Destroying Your Best Content
tldr: Gini Dietrich argues AI-driven repurposing dilutes high-value content, and offers a rule plus a quick test to detect the damage. Relevant to content lifecycle and repurposing discipline.
angle: When repurposing becomes erosion: protecting your flagship content from AI dilution.

title: A viral space moment fell in Nutella's lap. Here's how they capitalized.
tldr: A Nutella jar floating past Artemis II astronauts became a viral gift, and the brand's speed and restraint turned it into a model of real-time newsjacking. A neat storytelling case.
why_fresh: classic reactive-PR case, not a new idea
angle: The discipline of restraint: why stepping out of the spotlight made Nutella's moment work.

title: Манифест для креаторов
tldr: SETTERS report from the Shorty Awards on the end of big-brand, big-budget, glossy-production monopoly and the rise of creator-driven work. A signal on shifting power in content creation.
why_fresh: reports a power shift away from polished brand production
angle: The end of gloss: what the creator-led production shift means for brand content budgets.
